raw_consumer_statement:
I disputed a charge of {$4900.00} on XX/XX/year> for XXXX XXXX XXXX because the services provided were not as described and key amenities were unavailable without advance disclosure. 
After arrival I learned : The resort pool was closed for my entire stay The spa was closed The promised airport meet-and-assist and private lounge transfer did not occur Food and service were materially below the advertised five-star standard None of these limitations were disclosed before travel I am not an ocean swimmer and relied on the existence of a pool when selecting this remote resort. Had I known of the closures, I would not have booked. Because the property is on an isolated island with no alternatives, I had no ability to mitigate once there. 
I provided Citi with : The resorts written admission that the only alternative pool was occupied Booking confirmation showing no disclosure Emails confirming the arrival service failure My reliance statement Citi denied the dispute stating I received the services, which applies the wrong standard. The issue is whether services were AS DESCRIBED, not whether I physically stayed.
